idea-plugin.name: Mindmap - Call Graph Visualizer
idea-plugin.description:
Mindmap - Call Graph Visualizer

Visualize function call graphs as interactive mind maps inside your IDE. Place your cursor on any Kotlin function and press Alt / Option + G to instantly see its callers, callees, and function depth.

Features

  • Bidirectional Call Graph — Outbound calls + inbound callers with configurable depth (1–5)
  • Hierarchical Layout — Callers left, root center, callees right with crossing minimization
  • Mini-Map — VS Code-style overview with colored dots and click/drag navigation
  • Tree View — Collapsible tree with type-specific icons and section headers
  • Trace & Expand — Double-click to merge call graphs, Cmd+Click to re-center
  • Abstract Tracing — Traces through abstract/interface functions to concrete implementations
  • Hide/Unhide Nodes — Hide nodes with cascade logic, floating panel to restore
  • Smart Navigation — Arrow keys, history back/forward, click-to-navigate to source
  • Search & Filter — Filter nodes by name across both views
  • Hover Info Cards — Signature, file location, depth, and LOC count
  • Pan & Select Modes — Figma-style interaction with box selection and multi-node move
  • Smooth Zoom — Scroll wheel, pinch-to-zoom, and keyboard shortcuts at 60fps

Quick Start

  1. Place cursor inside any Kotlin function
  2. Press Alt / Option + G (or right-click → Generate Mindmap)
  3. Explore the interactive call graph in the Mindmap tool window

For shortcuts, detailed help, and more visit vishal2376.github.io/mindmap

Supports Kotlin (K1 & K2) on IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3–2026.1 and Android Studio Ladybug+.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.4

  • Collapse/Expand All — New buttons in tree view header to collapse or expand all nested nodes at once (#8)
  • Debug Mode — Toggle in settings to save plugin logs to a dedicated mindmap-debug.log file for bug reports; crashes are always logged (#9)
  • Tree View Info Badge — File name and LOC combined into a compact badge with improved text contrast
  • Tree Tooltip Removed — Disabled tooltip popup in tree view that was overlapping node text (#7)
  • Fixed: Multi-Node Drag — Selecting multiple nodes and dragging with trackpad now moves all selected nodes together (#10)
  • Fixed: Android Studio Navigation — Wrapped editor navigation in WriteIntentReadAction to fix crash on newer IntelliJ/Android Studio builds
  • Fixed: EDT Threading — Moved JSON serialization, HTML loading, and graph updates off the main thread for smoother UI
  • Fixed: Memory Leak — JCEF load handler is now properly removed on dispose
  • Removed Deprecated APIs — Replaced runReadAction and partiallyAppliedSymbol with current stable APIs

1.0.3

  • Redesigned Settings — New minimal inline settings panel with sliders, depth track indicators, reset-to-defaults, and clearer labels
  • Website & Feedback Buttons — Quick links to the plugin website and GitHub issues directly from the settings panel
  • Configurable Retrace Depth — Separate outbound/inbound depth controls for the trace (double-click) operation
  • Barycenter Crossing Minimization — Improved hierarchical layout reduces edge crossings for cleaner graphs
  • Node Position Persistence — Graph preserves node positions and zoom level after tracing
  • Fixed: Empty Blue Boxes — Cascade hiding no longer orphans nodes and leaves empty placeholders
  • Fixed: Abstract Function Navigation — Navigation now works correctly for abstract functions and focused-file graphs

1.0.2

  • Live Mini-Map — VS Code-style minimap (top-left) with colored node dots, viewport rectangle, click-to-pan, and drag-to-pan navigation
  • Hide/Unhide Nodes — Hover any node to reveal an eye button; hidden nodes cascade-hide disconnected children and preserve their layout position across retraces
  • Hidden Nodes Panel — Bottom-left panel auto-appears when nodes are hidden; lists all hidden nodes with one-click unhide for easy access
  • Settings Modal — Gear button opens a redesigned settings panel with outbound/inbound depth controls, visual depth track indicators, and clear descriptions
  • Configurable Depth — Adjust outbound (1-5) and inbound (1-5) trace depth from the settings modal; takes effect on next trace
  • Abstract Function Tracing — Traces through abstract/interface functions to their concrete implementations (up to 3)
  • Figma-style Zoom — Smooth pinch-to-zoom and scroll-zoom with requestAnimationFrame batching for 60fps performance
  • Straight-Line Edges — Replaced cubic bezier curves with straight lines for maximum rendering performance
  • Crossing Minimization — Improved hierarchical layout with hubsize sorting, edge minimization, block shifting, and shake-towards-leaves
  • Performance First — Always-on high-performance rendering (disabled shadows/smoothing/animations)
  • Figma-style Interaction — Toggle between Pan (H) and Select (V) modes
  • Incremental Trace — Double-click a node to merge its call graph into the view
  • Restart & Clear — New button/shortcut (R) to wipe graph data and free memory

1.0.1

  • Fixed Android Studio tool window icon collision
  • Added Keyboard Shortcuts help modal (?)
  • Keyboard focus persists correctly in Tool Window after code navigation

1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Interactive graph view with hierarchical layout
  • Tree view with SVG icons and continuous connectors
  • Box/marquee selection for multi-node movement
  • Right-click / middle-click drag for panning
  • Navigation history (back/forward)
  • Double-click trace with position preservation
  • Smart duplicate name detection with filename
  • Bidirectional analysis (outbound + inbound calls)
  • Large file optimization (300 node cap, batch limits)
  • Crash-proof error handling across all entry points
  • JCEF fallback panel for Android Studio
  • Catppuccin Mocha dark theme

action.description: Generate a call graph for the selected function
